notes.title: New logo-pill component in Figma — not used yet
notes.summary: The Figma file has a new component called _udc-nav-header_logo-pill (98×48) that pairs the brand vector with a 'ResMan' text label. The current nav-header is still using the older simple 24×44 logo without the label.
notes.decisionNeeded: Should the new logo-pill replace the simple logo, sit alongside it as a variant, or be left as a design exploration?
notes.autoPruneWhen: _udc-nav-header_logo-pill becomes a nested instance in the canonical udc-nav-header tree, or is deleted from the nav-header page.

notes.title: Logo has an Outline=True variant — not exposed in the docs
notes.summary: The _udc-nav-header_logo component set has two variants: Outline=False (the dark brand vector, currently used) and Outline=True (a light-fill version for dark surfaces). The doc-site only ships the dark variant; there's no prop or example for the light one.
notes.decisionNeeded: Is the light-fill variant part of the public contract (we add a prop + SVG export), or is it an internal-only variant we can ignore?
notes.autoPruneWhen: spec.json adds a prop that toggles the logo variant, or the Outline=True variant is removed from the Figma component set.

notes.title: Old bento components still sit on the Figma page
notes.summary: _udc-nav-header_dropdown and _udc-nav-header_nav-tile are still on the nav-header Figma page but aren't used by the canonical udc-nav-header anymore (the bento app-switcher was removed). They're orphans — leftovers from the previous design.
notes.decisionNeeded: Should these be deleted from the Figma page to keep it clean, or are they being kept as reference for something?
notes.autoPruneWhen: Both _udc-nav-header_dropdown and _udc-nav-header_nav-tile are deleted from the Figma page.

notes.title: Title-area chevron has no interaction — is that intended?
notes.summary: The title-area pill renders a downward chevron next to the page title. The previous design used it to open the bento app-switcher; that's gone now and no other popover is wired up. The chevron currently does nothing.
notes.decisionNeeded: Should the chevron be removed (it implies an interaction that doesn't exist), or is it intentional as a visual affordance for a future popover?

notes.title: Doc example uses a Material Symbol instead of the brand logo
notes.summary: Figma's logo is a hand-drawn brand-mark vector in surface/brand-bold (#023674). The doc-site examples use the 'apartment' Material Symbol as a stand-in because we don't have an SVG export and the brand color isn't in our imported token collections yet.
notes.decisionNeeded: Want to commit to an SVG export + token import so the docs match Figma, or leave the Material Symbol as the doc stand-in indefinitely?
notes.autoPruneWhen: The logo SVG is exported into the docs and the example HTML stops using the Material Symbol fallback.

notes.title: Header max-width is hardcoded — Figma uses a token we don't import
notes.summary: The header's outer Frame 31 in Figma binds max-width (1440px) to content-container/max-width-nav-container, but that token collection isn't in our import scope. The CSS bakes 1440 as a literal until the collection is added.
notes.decisionNeeded: Add the content-container collection to the token import scope (which would touch the import-figma-tokens pipeline), or accept the literal as a deliberate exception?
notes.autoPruneWhen: The content-container/max-width-nav-container token is imported into uds/tokens/*.css and nav-header.css consumes it.

notes.title: Authoring errors come from the badge page, not nav-header
notes.summary: When inspecting nav-header, Figma reports 'Component set for node has existing errors' messages. They actually originate from the udc-badge component set on the badge Figma page (the My Work pill nests a badge instance). The canonical udc-nav-header itself reads cleanly. The 🔴 status on the nav-header page in Figma will stay until the badge page errors are fixed.
notes.decisionNeeded: Schedule a fix-up on the badge page, or leave as-is for now? This isn't blocking docs.
notes.autoPruneWhen: The udc-badge component set on the badge page no longer reports authoring errors.
